I think that it is the root package of all packages of cps-ncmp-dmi-plugin project that should be renamed with something like `org.onap.cps.ncmp_dmi_plugin`.
`org.onap.cps.ncmp` is already a package prefix used for cps ncmp modules. Better to clearly make the distinction between these components at package root level.

That being said, such a refactoring is not part of this change and would need its own change.
But it seems that the client is using the rest template from its instance property.
please provide default value to these vars too.
please provide default value to these vars too. same for below var
lets have a quick chat in standup later today

I'm not sure about having default values here as ncp-dmi-plugin does not own these values. Some other external services or deployments are responsible for these. 

Then, providing some default value here is assuming something about another service. From a design point of view it make me thinking about a kind coupling (even if not a hard one by having the option to change).

Also providing a default value means giving preference to only one specific environment or deployment (local, docker compose, oom, ...) as what is going to work for one environment is not going to work for another. 

These are the reasons why I tend to leave these env variables for runtime only.

What do you think ?
Same comment as above.

suggestion: 
can use HttpHeaders.CONTENT_TYPE and MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE
suggestion: restTemplate.postForEntity instead of exchange - It makes it clear which http method is being called in the method name itself.
config class should not be in model package.

I am not sure if this class is the right place to create the bean. It looks like the class is mostly responsible for connection properties. But it is also creating resttemplate bean and breaks single responsibility principle.

It is recommended to use RestTemplateBuilder to create an instance of RestTemplate.

https://medium.com/@TimvanBaarsen/spring-boot-why-you-should-always-use-the-resttemplatebuilder-to-create-a-resttemplate-instance-d5a44ebad9e9

CPS_CORE must be running on some default values CPS_CORE_HOST and PORT. 

If this application.yml mentions those default values here, its in sync automatically. And if CPS_CORE changes those default value , here those values should be changed. Currently there are not separate configs for prod, dev and test so defalut configs are handy. I agree on above comments if we separate these profiles. 

Secondly in my opinion we should provide as much information before hand for config files as we have at the time of writing config files. And let admins decide if they want to go away with given information and use other alternatives. 

These are my 2 cents here.

Suggestion top level package for dmi-plugin classes : org.onap.cps.ncmp.dmi
Do as separated refactoring task: https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-499
will refactor when needed ie when more methods can added
it should be configurable

I agree, default values are a good idea for convenience
We can discuss at next stand-up

As an improvement from cps core, it would be good to replace field injection with constructor injection for such immutable dependencies.

Field injection is not recommended and has some drawbacks.
See this discussion for some explanations: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39890849/what-exactly-is-field-injection-and-how-to-avoid-it

Generally best practice is to to put exposed beans in configuration classes. Rather than adding it here, I feel it best suited in  CpsConfiguration if RestTemplate would be only used n the app for invoking cps rest apis.

If the scope of RestTemplate is wider that CPS configuration, then probably we could add that to common configs or main class.
